claim: REQ-005's MUST ('the second mount's own continuation MUST receive the settled outcome (rendered in ITS instance)') and its acceptance ('after settle BOTH instances render a settled state (the second via its own continuation, not a stuck pending) - asserted through the real components (e2e...)') pin a rendered behavior the unforked shared component makes unreachable, contradicting REQ-001's do-not-fork MUST. Every dispatch affordance in OrkyEntryActions is refused while the SHARED gate is busy: dq-action-preview is disabled={busy} (orky-entry-actions.tsx:355), the verdict buttons are disabled={busy} (:275,:279), and the escalation submit's refuse prop includes busy for both the click and the Enter path (:374, :241) - and busy derives from the same module-scope registry on EVERY mount, re-rendered via subscribeFlights before any subsequent real gesture, so a second e2e click/Enter can never fire while the first mount's flight is in the air. That refusal is itself pinned by the FROZEN e2e TEST-608 (orky-queue-actions.spec.ts:213 expects the submit DISABLED while dq-action-pending shows). So the spec's claim that 'REQ-005 makes [the loopback's unreachable two-mount vector] reachable and pins it' is false: the gesture-layer refusal keeps it unreachable by design; only the core-seam fan-out is real (already frozen, TEST-615). F10 cannot pass this acceptance without weakening the shared component's busy gates - which REQ-001 forbids forking, REQ-009 does not inventory, and frozen TEST-608 would trip. After settle the second instance renders IDLE (its phase never left idle; no dq-action-result/-error), not 'a settled state'.
fix: Rewrite REQ-005's acceptance to the reachable rendered claims: with both mounts live and a slow-CLI flight started from the queue mount, (a) exactly ONE bridge dispatch (stub argv-log length 1), (b) BOTH regions render dq-action-pending during the flight, (c) the PANE mount's dispatch affordances are disabled/refusing during the flight (the shared busy derivation - this IS the cross-instance protection users experience), (d) after settle the first mount renders the settled outcome and the second returns to idle with re-enabled controls. Keep the second-caller-continuation guarantee where it is actually pinned and testable: the core seam (frozen TEST-615), re-scoping the normative sentence to 'any caller whose gesture does reach withSingleFlight receives the settled outcome through its own continuation (core-seam guarantee, TEST-615)'. Delete the claim that F10 makes the rendered two-mount post-settle vector reachable.
location: 02-spec.md REQ-005 (acceptance + normative text); src/renderer/components/orky-entry-actions.tsx:241,355,374 (busy refusal gates); tests/e2e/orky-queue-actions.spec.ts:211-213 (frozen TEST-608 disabled-while-pending pin)
resolution: Resolved by the revised REQ-005 two-layer restatement. (a) The core-seam continuation guarantee is pinned where it is real: frozen TEST-615 byte-unchanged (orky-entry-actions-loopback.test.ts:78; its header :6-16 itself records the rendered two-mount vector as unreachable in its harness) - verified present and matching. (b) The rendered model now claims exactly the shipped behavior: one dispatch (stub argv-log length 1), dq-action-pending on BOTH mounts (busy derived from the shared registry at orky-entry-actions.tsx:79, re-rendered via subscribeFlights :64-65, showPending :303), the NON-INITIATING mount's affordances refusing on shared busy (preview disabled={busy} :353-355; escalation submit refuse incl. busy :374/:244-245 on click AND the Enter path :239-241; verdict buttons :274-279 - all re-verified against source), and post-settle initiating-renders-settle / non-initiating-returns-idle (its phase never left idle; registry release + notify re-enables controls). The phantom 'second mount renders the settle' claim is deleted and explicitly disclaimed ('never a phantom result... UNREACHABLE by design'). No REQ-001 contradiction remains: the acceptance requires zero shared-component change, and frozen TEST-608/TEST-585/TEST-615 are required byte-unchanged. The 4-step e2e is stageable with existing machinery: seedEscalatedProject + seedPlugin slow-CLI stub + argv stub logs (orky-queue-actions.spec.ts) plus the palette-to-picker bound-pane flow with region-scoped locators (orky-pane.spec.ts TEST-442); the answer toggle is not busy-gated, so the pane's form can be opened to observe the disabled submit mid-flight.

claim: REQ-002's acceptance vehicle is not implementable in this repo's harnesses: it requires counting registryDetail calls (0 on answer-open, exactly 1 on submit) 'with the component-lifecycle-real pane mount (CONV-031) and a spied registryDetail', but the unit harness is node-env with NO jsdom and cannot mount a component at all (the exact fact CONV-033 records and orky-entry-actions-structure.test.ts:2-6 restates), and e2e cannot spy the bundled api module's registryDetail (the module closure over the contextBridge surface is unreachable from page-context evaluate; F8's own frozen e2e TEST-608 proved binding-is-a-read only indirectly via empty CLI stub logs and never counted registryDetail calls). F8 pinned the pull-count discipline at the CORE seam with an injected pull (frozen TEST-588 'supplied escalationId binds AS-IS with ZERO pulls' and TEST-591 'the F10-style target makes exactly ONE - the submit-time verification only'). As written, the tests phase must either fail to implement the acceptance or mislabel a core-seam test as a 'pane mount' one - the CONV-033 class of citing a harness that cannot do what is claimed.
fix: Restate REQ-002's acceptance as the honest pair (the CONV-033-sanctioned split F8 already used): (1) pull-count discipline at the core seam driving the exact pane-shaped target (supplied escalationId; omitted-id refusal) - already frozen as TEST-588/TEST-591, cite them as the count half; (2) the rendered halves at e2e - dq-action-answer-target text equals the expanded row's first open data-escalation-id, the changed-world submit refusal renders the /changed|re-?open/i message with zero dispatch (the TEST-612 fixture-rewrite technique), and the null-id fixture renders the shared bind refusal with zero dispatch. Drop the spied-call-count-through-a-real-mount phrasing.
location: 02-spec.md REQ-002 (acceptance: 'component-lifecycle-real pane mount (CONV-031) and a spied registryDetail')
resolution: Resolved by the revised REQ-002 acceptance, re-vehicled per CONV-033 exactly as prescribed. Count half: frozen TEST-588/TEST-591 cited byte-unchanged - re-verified at orky-entry-actions-core.test.ts:191 (supplied escalationId binds AS-IS with zero pulls; the structural id beats status.detail free text) and :274 (F10-style target makes exactly ONE pull, the submit-time verification only; omitted-id style makes two). Structural half: a node-harness source scan of OrkyPane.tsx (no registryDetail reference; escalationId sourced from the held detail entry) - the established structural-scan technique this repo's node-env suites already use. Rendered halves: e2e via testids + empty-CLI-stub-log assertions, incl. the TEST-612 fixture-rewrite technique (verified at orky-queue-actions.spec.ts:367) applied to the pane mount, the null-id fixture refusal, and the free-text-different-id vector. Every stated vehicle exists in this repo's harnesses; the spied-registryDetail-through-a-real-mount phrasing is gone.

claim: CONV-008 violation in the scheduled doc sweep: F10 falsifies the standing strictly-read-only claims about the OrkyPane surface, but REQ-009's stated CONV-008 sweep covers only stale 'F10 will/future call' phrasings - the read-only claim's OTHER phrasings escape it. docs/features/orky-pane.md:8 asserts 'Strictly READ-only: no .orky/ write, no CLI, no orkyAction:* call, no registry...' and OrkyPane.tsx:11-14's header asserts 'Strictly READ-only (REQ-013): no action affordance, no mutation call' - after F10 the pane composes answer/record-gate dispatch and the rooted capture opener, so both claims become false, yet neither contains 'F10 will' nor 'future call' and neither is caught by the stated sweep pattern. CONV-008 requires grepping the whole docs tree (plus CLAUDE.md and .orky/baseline/) for EVERY phrasing of the changed claim, never only the enumerated files/phrasings. (Verified: no test pins these two texts - docs-feature-0009.test.ts pins no read-only phrase - so the update is free; the defect is that the spec's sweep as stated will not find them and doc-sync would ship docs contradicting shipped behavior.)
fix: Extend REQ-009's doc-sync sweep to the read-only-claim phrasings and state the pattern per CONV-023/CONV-008, e.g. rg -i 'read.?only|no action affordance|no mutation call' over docs/**, CLAUDE.md, .orky/baseline/ and src/**/OrkyPane* comments, with an explicit disposition for docs/features/orky-pane.md:8 (rewrite to 'writes only via the shared F8 action layer and F12 capture - no dispatch/capture logic of its own'), the orky-pane.md 'Forward compatibility (F10)' section (retire the future tense), and the OrkyPane.tsx:11-14 header (re-express as the TEST-433-amended scope: composes the shared region, owns no raw CLI/mutation/dispatch call).
location: 02-spec.md REQ-009 (doc-pins bullet, CONV-008 sweep scope); docs/features/orky-pane.md:8; src/renderer/components/OrkyPane.tsx:11-14
resolution: Resolved by the revised REQ-009 doc-pins bullet: the CONV-008 sweep now states TWO patterns - (1) the stale future-tense pattern and (2) the read-only-CLAIM pattern rg -i 'read.?only|no action affordance|no mutation call' - with a noise-disposition rule, and names the two MANDATORY corrections: docs/features/orky-pane.md:8-9 ('Strictly READ-only: no .orky/ write, no CLI, no orkyAction:* call, no registry mutation' - verified present at those lines) and the OrkyPane.tsx:13-14 header ('Strictly READ-only (REQ-013): no action affordance, no mutation call' - verified present). The acceptance now requires 'no un-dispositioned pattern-(2) hit that describes the pane's write surface' and that both named texts no longer claim strict read-only. Pin-free claim re-verified 2026-07-02: docs-feature-0009.test.ts and docs-feature-0012.test.ts contain no read.?only/no-action-affordance/no-mutation-call match, and no other suite pins either text (the 'strictly read'-matching suites - decision-queue-panel-structure TEST-353 (the permitting precedent), orky-notify-wiring-structure (scans only the observer module), orky-root-engine, orky-tracker - all guard OTHER surfaces). Residual defect in the repair: the bullet's premise '(no .orky/baseline/ exists - decision #6)' is FALSE (the baseline exists, committed 2026-06-29) - filed separately as FINDING-006 (LOW; verified zero missed corrections today: the baseline's read-only phrasings all describe main-process/IPC read surfaces F10 does not falsify).

claim: REQ-002's zero-display-pull MUST is stated unconditionally but is false for the omitted-id branch the same REQ mandates: when the first open escalation carries no usable id, escalationId MUST be omitted and 'the shared bind path then refuses honestly' - and that shared bind path (orky-entry-actions-core.ts:140-161) makes exactly ONE display-time registryDetail pull before refusing. Resolved decision #2 even says so ('one pull, the established F8 behavior'), so the spec contradicts itself between the decision and the requirement sentence. A literal enforcement of the MUST (e.g. a structural test that answer-open in the pane never triggers registryDetail) would fail on REQ-002's own null-id acceptance fixture, or worse, tempt an implementer to pre-check around the shared bind path - which REQ-002 separately and correctly bans.
fix: Scope the sentence to the supplied-id case: 'For rows that supply an escalationId, the answer path MUST make ZERO display-time registryDetail pulls (the id binds as-is) and EXACTLY ONE pull per submit (the verification); rows that omit the id follow the established F8 path (one display-time bind pull ending in the honest refusal, no dispatch).'
location: 02-spec.md REQ-002 (normative text, 'Consequently the F10 answer path MUST make ZERO display-time registryDetail pulls')
resolution: Resolved by the revised REQ-002 normative text, scoped exactly as prescribed: 'For rows that SUPPLY an escalationId, the answer path MUST make ZERO display-time registryDetail pulls (the supplied id binds as-is) and EXACTLY ONE registryDetail pull per submit', with the omitted-id branch restated as the established F8 path unmodified ('one display-time bind pull ending in the shared honest refusal, no dispatch') plus an explicit ban on a pane-side pre-check to save that pull. Now consistent with resolved decision #2 and with orky-entry-actions-core.ts:137-139 (supplied-id) vs :140-162 (omitted-id) - the self-contradiction is gone and a literal structural enforcement no longer conflicts with the null-id acceptance fixture.

claim: REQ-009's acceptance is unverifiable as stated: the stated ripgrep pattern (whose 'read-?only' alternation is very broad) matches 28 files under tests/** today, of which the spec dispositions about 10 by name; the other 18 hits (e.g. tests/docs-feature-0006.test.ts:29, tests/renderer/decision-queue-panel-structure.test.ts:142-159 TEST-353, tests/renderer/orky-notify-wiring-structure.test.ts:36, tests/e2e/orky-status.spec.ts:86, tests/main/orky-action-dispatcher.test.ts:316-328, tests/shared/keybindings-capture-orky-work.test.ts:64-76) are left silently un-dispositioned, so 'finds no un-dispositioned hit' cannot pass and a tests-phase checker cannot distinguish 'read and found irrelevant' from 'missed'. (I re-ran the grep and read the extra hits: all are read-only prose in unrelated suites or guards F10 provably does not trip - no missed frozen guard - so this is a precision defect in the acceptance, not a missed supersession.)
fix: Either add a one-line bulk disposition naming the remaining hit files as pattern-noise ('match only read-only prose / scopes F10 does not touch; verified green byte-unchanged'), or narrow the stated pattern (drop the bare read-?only alternation in favor of the F9/F10 surface literals per CONV-037's keying rule) and restate the acceptance against the narrowed pattern.
location: 02-spec.md REQ-009 (acceptance: 'a repo grep with the stated pattern finds no un-dispositioned hit')
resolution: Resolved by the revised REQ-009: the pattern is narrowed to the F9/F10 surface literals per CONV-037's keying rule ('orky-pane-row-actions|OrkyEntryActions|openOrkyCapture|orky-entry-actions|F10', rg -i over tests/**), the bare read-?only alternation is dropped (its claim-sweep duty moved to the doc-pins bullet's pattern (2)), and the acceptance is restated against the narrowed pattern with a stop-and-disposition rule for any new hit file. Re-ran the stated grep 2026-07-02: exactly 14 hit files, matching one-to-one the 14 dispositions named in the REQ (incl. orky-capture-slice.test.ts as hit-file #14 and the pattern-noise bullet's four files). The acceptance is now mechanically checkable.

claim: The FINDING-003 repair rests on a false factual premise, narrowing the CONV-008 sweep below the convention's stated floor: resolved decision #6 asserts '.orky/compliance/, .orky/baseline/ absent' and REQ-009's pattern-(2) scope repeats '(no .orky/baseline/ exists - decision #6)', but .orky/baseline/ EXISTS in this repo (committed 2026-06-29 - before the spec's 'verified 2026-07-02' stamp - with architecture.md, inferred-spec.md, traceability.json; only .orky/compliance/ is genuinely absent). CONV-008 explicitly names .orky/baseline/ in the retire-a-claim sweep scope ('grep the WHOLE docs/ tree (plus CLAUDE.md and .orky/baseline/)'), so the stated sweep skips a mandated location on a false 'verified' claim. Material impact verified nil TODAY: the baseline's five read-only phrasings (architecture.md:31,87,91,119,135) all describe main-process/IPC read surfaces F10 does not falsify (the :87 'read-only' subsystem claim already carries its F7 CONV-008 correction note, and :119's read-only registry:detail/rootChanged surfaces stay read-only after F10), and decision #6's operative conclusion (no compliance controls to encode) still holds - hence LOW, but the spec's false absence assertion and the under-scoped sweep must not survive into doc-sync.
fix: Correct decision #6 to claim only what is true (.orky/compliance/ absent - no controls to encode; the .orky/baseline/ dir EXISTS) and add .orky/baseline/ to REQ-009's pattern-(2) sweep scope per CONV-008, dispositioning its five current read-only hits (architecture.md:31,87,91,119,135) as noise: all describe main-process/IPC read surfaces (tracker/watcher path, registry:detail/rootChanged, driveStatus) that remain read-only after F10.
location: 02-spec.md resolved decision #6 + REQ-009 doc-pins bullet ('no .orky/baseline/ exists'); C:/dev/Termhalla/.orky/baseline/ (exists: architecture.md, inferred-spec.md, traceability.json)
resolution: Fixed (coordinator, pre-freeze prose correction): decision #6 + REQ-009 corrected — .orky/baseline/ DOES exist and is now in the CONV-008 sweep scope, but its read-only phrasings describe main-process/IPC read surfaces F10 does not falsify, so no mandatory correction; the false absent claim removed.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyPane.tsx:189 (flex:'none' on the orky-pane-row-actions slot); tests/e2e/orky-pane-actions.spec.ts:261-277 (TEST-632 narrow-tile vector)
claim: REQ-007's wrap mechanism is structurally defeated in the pane mount: the orky-pane-row-actions slot span carries flex:'none' (flex 0 0 auto), so its flex base size is the region's max-content width and it can never shrink below it — the shared region's internal flexWrap rows (orky-entry-actions.tsx:219,:266,:361) therefore never engage inside the pane. In a tile narrower than the region's intrinsic width (~260-280px: 'answer…'+'next?'+'resume in terminal' plus gaps, or the open form's target-span + 120px input flex-basis + submit) the region overflows the tile horizontally, and the whole features list gains a horizontal scrollbar (the scroll container's overflowY:auto computes overflow-x to auto) — the controls become scroll-to-reach instead of wrapping, exactly the drawer-rigidity class the spec's 'may WRAP within the row' clause exists to prevent. The pinning test cannot catch this: TEST-632 resizes the WINDOW to 720px — a tile wider than even the 340px drawer the spec contrasts against — and its clipping assertion compares box.x+box.width to window.innerWidth, not the pane TILE's bounds; Playwright's toBeVisible/boundingBox also ignore ancestor overflow clipping, so the acceptance's 'no clipped/unreachable control' claim is pinned by a check that passes even when the control is clipped. A requirement pinned only by this shallow vector is itself a defect.
fix: Let the slot shrink so the shared region's own wrap engages: replace flex:'none' with flex:'0 1 auto' plus minWidth:0 on the orky-pane-row-actions span (the region then wraps gracefully down to its widest single control, ~120px). Strengthen the e2e vector to a genuinely narrow TILE: split the workspace (or shrink the pane's tile) below ~300px and assert each dq-action-* control's boundingBox sits inside the PANE TILE's boundingBox, not window.innerWidth. propose CONV: A narrow-layout acceptance for a control region inside a user-resizable tile MUST assert clipping against the tile's own bounding box at a genuinely narrow tile width — never against window.innerWidth — because Playwright visibility/boundingBox checks ignore ancestor overflow clipping.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback + implement fix, re-verified against current code 2026-07-03. The orky-pane-row-actions slot dropped flex:none for minWidth:0 (OrkyPane.tsx:195 — default flex 0 1 auto, shrinkable, so the shared region's own flexWrap rows engage; the feature row itself also wraps, :164), pinned structurally by TEST-638 (orky-pane-actions-loopback.test.ts, green in the 1416/0 gate). The prescribed stronger e2e vector exists as TEST-646 (orky-pane-actions-loopback.spec.ts): a ~225px REAL mosaic tile (two rightward editor splits of a 900px window, tile width asserted <320), every dq-action-* control asserted inside the PANE TILE's own bounding box (never window.innerWidth), plus a zero-horizontal-scroll-debt check on the features list closed AND with the form open. WITNESSED GREEN at this re-verification run (2026-07-03, 3/3 loopback spec passed against the implement-gate build). The proposed CONV (tile-relative narrow-layout assertions) remains in fix for doc-sync promotion.

location: src/renderer/components/OrkyPane.tsx:268-298 (pane header: no flexWrap; rigid nowrap spans; inject is the last item)
claim: The new inject affordance can be pushed clean out of the tile with no way to reach it: the pane header is a no-wrap flex row whose middle spans are non-shrinkable nowrap items (source, 'needs you', 'failed', and 'active: {slug}' — a real slug like '0010-orky-pane-inline-actions' is ~170px at fontSize 11; only projectName and the stalled span are ellipsis-shrinkable), and orky-pane-inject is the LAST flex item. In a realistic split tile (~350-400px) with a busy project — precisely the state where the badges and active-feature span all render, and where a user most wants to inject follow-up work — the '+ work' button overflows past the tile edge and is clipped by the mosaic tile, with NO scroll access (the header sits outside the features scroll container, overflow visible). REQ-003 makes discoverability of this affordance a MUST-level concern but the spec encoded no narrow-tile reachability requirement for the header (REQ-007's layout clause covers only the row actions region), and TEST-634 exercises inject only at the default window size — so the affordance silently vanishes at common tile widths with nothing pinning it.
fix: Make the header resilient: add flexWrap:'wrap' to the header row (the inject wraps to a second header line) or make the rigid informational spans shrinkable (minWidth:0 + overflow hidden + ellipsis, as projectName and stalledTop already are) so the inject button is never pushed out of the tile; add an e2e vector asserting orky-pane-inject's boundingBox stays inside the pane tile's boundingBox at a narrow tile width. Encode the header-reachability requirement in the spec via loopback so the fix is pinned, not incidental.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback + implement fix, re-verified against current code 2026-07-03. The pane header row now declares flexWrap:'wrap' (OrkyPane.tsx:278 — the inject affordance wraps to a second header line instead of clipping off-tile; inject stays last with marginLeft:'auto', :305). Pinned structurally by TEST-639 (orky-pane-actions-loopback.test.ts, green) and rendered by TEST-646, whose fixture deliberately renders the rigid header spans (source + needs-you + 'active: esc-feature' via withActive) in the ~225px tile and asserts orky-pane-inject inside the tile's own bounding box — WITNESSED GREEN 2026-07-03. The reachability requirement is encoded as frozen pins per the ESC-001 decision (the spec file is freeze-locked; the pin, not prose, is what prevents regression).

location: 02-spec.md REQ-006; src/renderer/components/orky-entry-actions.tsx:338-343 (mode-flip disarm effect); src/renderer/components/use-open-focus-restore.ts:23-29 (restore path with no fallback)
claim: The CONV-046 mode-flip disarm destroys the user's typed draft with zero user-visible signal: when a background refresh flips target.reason while the user is mid-typing (the escalation resolved elsewhere — the queue mount, another agent, the feedback loop applying a queued decision), the form unmounts and the typed decision/evidence clear SILENTLY — no message says the escalation changed or that the draft was discarded; the only cue is the row's tiny reason span changing text. Closing the form is the right call (CONV-046 — never repurpose the focus substrate without a gesture), but doing it silently is a trust and data-loss gap: the user is left staring at where their half-typed answer just was, with no explanation and no recovery. The spec itself mandated clear-without-notice (REQ-006) and frozen TEST-630 now pins the disarm effect body as 'toasts NOTHING', so a notice is unimplementable without a deliberate test amendment — the gap is spec-originated, not an implementer miss. Aggravating: on an escalation→null/stalled flip the answer TOGGLE unmounts along with the form, so useOpenFocusRestore's restore target (prev = the toggle) is no longer in the document and no fallbackSelector is supplied — keyboard focus falls to body, stranding a keyboard user mid-flow (REQ-006's acceptance only asserts focus is not moved INTO a field; it never says where focus may land).
fix: Follow-up spec+tests amendment (CONV-019 discipline, not a silent edit): on a mode-flip disarm, report through the existing store toast chokepoint — the CONV-034 never-silently-dropped signal, no new testid — e.g. 'the escalation for <slug> changed — the answer form closed and your typed draft was discarded', and supply a fallback focus target (the row's disclosure button or the actions region) for the flip vector where the captured opener unmounted, so focus never lands on body; amend TEST-630's no-toast pin and TEST-637 atomically in the same change. propose CONV: A data-driven disarm/close that discards user-typed input MUST surface a user-visible notice through a never-silently-dropped signal and MUST NOT strand keyboard focus on body.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback + implement fix, re-verified against current code 2026-07-03. The CONV-046 disarm now (a) reports a draft-discarding flip through the store toast chokepoint on the never-suppressed 'error' kind, guarded on a NON-EMPTY typed decision/evidence (orky-entry-actions.tsx:362-369 — a no-draft flip verified to toast NOTHING: the pushToast sits inside the emptiness guard), and (b) re-anchors keyboard focus onto the region (tabIndex -1, :392) ONLY when activeElement collapsed to body/null (:373-375) — the re-anchor runs after the unmounted form's useOpenFocusRestore cleanup in the same commit, so the escalation→human-review flip (where the restore lands focus on the surviving toggle) and any user-intentional focus are never yanked; only the escalation→null collapse re-anchors. TEST-630's no-toast/no-focus bans were amended atomically per CONV-019 with a header naming the supersession; TEST-642 pins the draft guard + error kind + fallback structurally (green); e2e TEST-647 (toasts deliberately NOT enabled — pins the never-suppressed kind end-to-end; focus asserted not on body; zero dispatch) WITNESSED GREEN 2026-07-03. Residual examined and judged below filing threshold: a reason flip landing while a submitted flight is still in the air (or React-batched with its success settle) shows the draft-discarded notice for text that was in fact dispatched — the message stays literally accurate (the feature changed; the form closed) and the success still renders, so no data or dispatch harm. The proposed CONV remains in fix for doc-sync promotion.

claim: The CONV-046 disarm clears the typed decision/evidence but NOT the hook's escalation binding, and errorView's unbound branch is not mode-gated: on a row whose escalation binding FAILED (e.g. the null-id honest refusal, TEST-635 vector 2), a data-driven reason flip disarms the form, and the next explicit re-open — now mounting the HumanReviewForm — renders the STALE role=alert error (kind 'escalation-unbound', message about the escalation carrying no id) beside the verdict form. The stale red alert describes a surface the open form no longer is; REQ-006's acceptance phrase 'a fresh binding' holds only for the escalation-mode re-open (toggleAnswer re-runs bindAnswer) — the flipped-mode re-open reuses the stale failed binding. No dispatch/correctness impact (binding is unused by the human-review submit path; verdict buttons work), so LOW: a misleading, CONV-001-class stale error surface on an edge chain (unbindable id + background flip + re-open).
fix: Gate errorView's unbound branch on the escalation mode (answerOpen && mode === 'escalation' && binding !== null && binding !== 'pending' && !binding.ok), and/or clear the stale binding at disarm time via a hook-exposed resetBinding() pure setter — never a bind PULL from the effect (that would trip the gesture-tying pins). Add the vector (failed-bind row, flip, re-open shows NO stale alert) to the modeflip/e2e coverage.
resolution: Resolved at the implement fix, re-verified against current code 2026-07-03. The errorView unbound branch is now escalation-mode-gated: answerOpen && mode === 'escalation' && binding !== null && binding !== 'pending' && !binding.ok (orky-entry-actions.tsx:333) — a FAILED binding surviving a mode flip can no longer resurface as a stale role=alert beside a re-opened verdict form of the other mode; an escalation-mode re-open re-binds fresh via toggleAnswer → bindAnswer (:386). Pinned by TEST-643 (orky-entry-actions-modeflip-loopback.test.ts, the option-B mode-gate branch; also verifies the disarm never PULLS a binding), green in the gate run and my re-run.

location: src/renderer/components/orky-entry-actions.tsx:389,:397 (armedMode === mode render guards), :348 (the arming point); tests/renderer/orky-entry-actions-modeflip.test.ts:63-77 (TEST-630 pins only the effect)
claim: REQ-006's load-bearing half is unpinned. The armedMode render guard (armedMode === mode on both form conditions plus the gesture-only setArmedMode(mode) arming point) is what actually prevents the flipped mode's focus-on-mount form from MOUNTING in the pre-effect commit: the disarm useEffect alone cannot, because a mounted child's useOpenFocusRestore effect runs BEFORE the parent's disarm effect in the same commit, so without the guard the wrong-mode form mounts and steals focus for a commit before unmounting. TEST-630 pins only the disarm effect (existence, clears, purity) and TEST-637 cannot observe the transient (its post-flip assertions settle identically with or without the guard). A future refactor deleting the guard while keeping the effect leaves every F10 test green and silently reintroduces a sub-second FINDING-022-class focus steal. To be clear: the implementer's armedMode approach itself is SOUND, not a smell — both halves are load-bearing (the guard alone would leave stale answerOpen state that remounts focus-stealing on a flip-back A->B->A; the effect alone loses the pre-paint window) — the defect is purely the missing regression pin on the guard.
fix: Amend TEST-630 (an F10-owned frozen suite — via the sanctioned tests loopback) to also pin the render-guard structure: both mode-keyed form render conditions contain the literal 'armedMode === mode', and 'setArmedMode(' appears exactly once, inside the toggleAnswer gesture handler and inside no useEffect span.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback, re-verified against current code 2026-07-03. The load-bearing armedMode === mode render guard is now regression-pinned by TEST-641 (orky-entry-actions-modeflip-loopback.test.ts): the literal guard required on BOTH mode-keyed form render conditions (verified present at orky-entry-actions.tsx:424,:432), setArmedMode( required to have exactly ONE call site, inside toggleAnswer (:381) and inside NO useEffect span — deleting the guard or arming it data-driven now fails this suite even though TEST-630/637 settle identically. Green in the gate run and in my re-run.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-009 (acceptance: 'yields exactly the 14 files dispositioned above and no other')
claim: REQ-009's inventory acceptance is mechanically unsatisfiable as restated after the FINDING-005 repair: the stated narrowed grep (rg -i 'orky-pane-row-actions|OrkyEntryActions|openOrkyCapture|orky-entry-actions|F10' over tests/**) now returns 18 files — the 14 dispositioned PLUS F10's own four new suites (tests/docs-feature-0010.test.ts, tests/renderer/orky-entry-actions-modeflip.test.ts, tests/renderer/orky-pane-actions-structure.test.ts, tests/e2e/orky-pane-actions.spec.ts), which unavoidably contain the surface literals they pin — so 'yields exactly the 14 files ... and no other (a new hit file is a new, un-dispositioned guard — stop and disposition it)' can never pass once the tests phase lands, and the stop-and-disposition rule fires spuriously on the feature's own files. Verified by running the stated pattern against the working tree. This is the same acceptance-precision class FINDING-005 fixed; the repair overlooked self-hits. No missed frozen guard exists (the four extra hits are F10's own suites).
fix: One-line, pin-free spec-prose amendment: restate the check as 'exactly the 14 dispositioned files plus the test files this feature itself adds (named)' — or exclude the feature's own suites from the sweep by construction.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback, re-verified 2026-07-03. The acceptance's own stop-and-disposition rule was exercised and recorded: 04-tests.md dispositions the inventory as exactly the spec's 14 files + F10's 4 phase-4 suites + the loopback's 4 pattern-hit files (22), and TEST-645 (docs-feature-0010-loopback.test.ts) pins the EXACT 22-file set mechanically so the count can never silently drift — re-ran the stated pattern 2026-07-03: exactly the pinned 22 files, no other. The frozen spec prose ('exactly the 14') stands un-amended (spec-freeze); the tests-phase disposition + TEST-645 pin serve as the restatement the fix prescribed.

claim: The cross-feature F8 ledger edit (FINDING-022 -> resolved) is substantively APPROPRIATE — REQ-006 mandates recording it, CONV-012 co-ownership covers the shared-file edit, and the resolution text accurately names the mechanism and real pins (TEST-630 / TEST-637 both exist and pin what is claimed; the cited F8 frozen suites verified green) — but its resolvedAt '2026-07-02T18:30:00.000Z' is a hand-synthesized round instant that predates the fix's existence: F10's tests gate passed 2026-07-03T00:27:40Z and implement 2026-07-03T00:51:00Z, and every other resolution stamp in both ledgers carries a real millisecond clock reading (e.g. F10 FINDING-006 at 23:58:35.713Z). An audit-ledger timestamp that corresponds to no real event degrades the trail it exists to provide.
fix: Correct resolvedAt to the actual edit instant. propose CONV: A findings-ledger status/resolution edit MUST stamp its timestamp fields from the real clock at edit time — never a hand-typed or estimated instant.
note: Re-verified 2026-07-03T02:45:19.278Z: F8 FINDING-022 resolvedAt still reads the hand-synthesized 2026-07-02T18:30:00.000Z. Stays OPEN as the accepted residual per 04-tests.md (nothing executable to pin): doc-sync must correct the stamp to a real clock instant and should promote the proposed CONV (ledger timestamps from the real clock at edit time — this re-verification stamped its own edits that way).

location: NUL (repo root — tracked since F9 commit 796c11b; appears as a phantom deletion in the F10 working diff)
claim: The F10 working diff carries an unrelated phantom deletion of a tracked file literally named NUL (a Windows reserved device name, almost certainly a stray '> NUL' redirect artifact committed with F9). Git on Windows cannot stat the path, so every Windows worktree shows it permanently 'deleted': every diff — this feature's included — is polluted, a blanket 'git add -A' feature commit would delete it as an undocumented rider, and fresh Windows checkouts are dirty-by-default. Not an F10 code defect, but it rides F10's diff and must be dispositioned deliberately rather than silently.
fix: Commit the deletion deliberately (git rm --cached NUL) as a named hygiene change, not a silent rider inside the F10 feature commit. propose CONV: No tracked path may use a Windows-reserved device name (NUL, CON, PRN, AUX, COM1-COM9, LPT1-LPT9) — treat such a path as a scope/hygiene check failure.
note: Re-verified 2026-07-03T02:45:19.278Z: the reserved-name path NUL is still tracked (git ls-files). On this checkout git status currently shows no phantom deletion (local git tolerates the stat), but the hazard for other Windows worktrees and the rider-deletion risk stand. Stays OPEN as the standing backlog item per ESC-001: commit git rm --cached NUL deliberately as a named hygiene change, never inside the F10 feature commit; the proposed CONV remains for doc-sync.

claim: CONV-008 residue inside REQ-009's own swept scope: the pattern-(2) read-only-claim sweep still leaves two claims about the decision-queue drawer that F8's composition falsified and that F10's own CHANGELOG entry now directly contradicts ('the SAME shared actions region the decision-queue drawer uses'): CLAUDE.md:216 calls the drawer a read-only what-needs-me-now queue, and docs/features/decision-queue.md:34 claims the surface never invokes an Orky CLI or orkyAction:* method — but the drawer rows have mounted the write-capable OrkyEntryActions region since F8. F10's noise-disposition rule (hits about unrelated surfaces) lets them escape as F6-scoped prose, yet they describe the same composed Orky write surface the sweep exists to keep honest, and CLAUDE.md now states 'read-only queue' two rows above the row documenting that queue's write-capable actions.
fix: In F10's doc-sync change, correct both phrasings the same way the orky-pane row/header were corrected: the drawer COMPOSES the shared F8 action region (write-capable via F7's bridges) while F6's own files still own no dispatch of their own.
resolution: Resolved at the implement/doc fix, re-verified against current text 2026-07-03. CLAUDE.md:216 now describes the drawer as composing the write-capable OrkyEntryActions region since feature 0008 (while F6's own files still own no dispatch), and docs/features/decision-queue.md's claim is rewritten as 'Composed write surface (since feature 0008), chrome-only' with the F6-owns-no-write-of-its-own scope explicit — the same correction shape the orky-pane row/header received. Pinned by TEST-644 (docs-feature-0010-loopback.test.ts), green in the gate run and my re-run.

claim: The frozen e2e acceptance suite cannot go green against a behaviorally-correct implementation, and it had never been executed against the implementation before this review (the last Playwright run predates the F10 spec file; the node-app gate profile runs vitest only). I ran tests/e2e/orky-pane-actions.spec.ts against the implement-gate build (out/ of 2026-07-02 20:49): 5 failed / 1 passed, deterministic across 3 attempts each. TEST-632 (:204), TEST-635 (:432), TEST-636 (:481) and TEST-637 (:566) all die on expect(readLog(gatekeeperLog)).toEqual([]) receiving [["contract"]]: the startup contract handshake (services.ts:62 -> verifyOrkyContract -> gatekeeper cli.js contract, commit 0dc4c54 of 2026-07-01 - BEFORE both the F8 and F10 tests phases) unconditionally invokes the stub gatekeeper on every launch with ORKY_PLUGIN_DIR set, so every raw-emptiness assertion on that argv log is unsatisfiable by construction. Consequences for the acceptance evidence: REQ-005's ENTIRE rendered two-mount model never executed (TEST-636 failed at :481 before the flight started - shared pending on both mounts, mid-flight refusal, argv-length-1 and idle-return are all unwitnessed); REQ-002's changed-world vector never executed (TEST-635 died at :432, before the fixture rewrite); TEST-632's answer dispatch/argv pin/CONV-041-044/narrow-tile assertions (:216-277) never executed; and the co-owned frozen F8 e2e pins the spec REQUIRES to pass byte-unchanged (TEST-608 at orky-queue-actions.spec.ts:185/:230, TEST-612 at :403) carry the identical defect, so the REQ-002/REQ-005 acceptance clause 'frozen TEST-608/TEST-612 pass byte-unchanged' is unsatisfiable as written. Where evidence DOES exist the implementation looks correct: TEST-634 (inject, REQ-003) passed fully, and TEST-637 passed every CONV-046 disarm assertion (:559-563 - both mounts' forms unmount, focus untouched, zero dispatch) before dying on the log assertion. This is a tests-phase defect (the assertions mis-model shipped startup behavior), invisible at the tests gate because the e2e is outside every gate command and its RED state was attributed wholly to the missing UI.
fix: Loopback to the tests phase (ADR-009, never a silent implement-phase edit): amend the argv-log assertions to disregard the unconditional startup handshake - e.g. have readLog (or a dedicated helper) filter entries whose argv[0] === 'contract', or assert on the action-subset of the log - in orky-pane-actions.spec.ts, and amend the co-owned F8 e2e (TEST-608/612) atomically in the same change per CONV-012 with a header naming F10; then RUN the full spec file green against the built app and record that run as review evidence. propose CONV: An assertion that a shared stub/spy saw ZERO invocations MUST be scoped to the actions under test (filtering unconditional startup/handshake traffic to the same stub), and a frozen acceptance suite that no gate command executes MUST be run green at least once against the implementation before its feature's review gate may close.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback (CONV-019, CONV-012), witnessed at this re-verification. All gatekeeper argv-log reads in the F10 e2e (orky-pane-actions.spec.ts, 9 sites, TEST-632/633/635/636/637 tagged), the co-owned F8 e2e (orky-queue-actions.spec.ts, 7 sites, TEST-608/609/611/612 tagged — 609/611 carried the identical defect class and were amended in the same sweep) and F8's loopback e2e (TEST-620) now go through readActionLog, which asserts every excluded entry IS exactly ['contract'] (real traffic can never be silently dropped — an unexpected contract-prefixed argv fails loudly) and returns the log without the startup handshake; each file carries the CONV-019 header naming F10's ESC-001 loopback and preserving the pinned intent (zero user-gesture dispatch until a gesture; exactly-one dispatch per episode). RUN EVIDENCE (2026-07-03, against the implement-gate build — out/ newer than every src file): orky-pane-actions.spec.ts 5 passed / 1 failed, the sole failure TEST-633 on FINDING-017's environment class (claude-log.txt never appears; node-pty binding absent), NOT the handshake — REQ-005's full rendered two-mount model (TEST-636), REQ-002's changed-world vector (TEST-635), TEST-632 and TEST-637 all green; orky-pane-actions-loopback.spec.ts 3/3 green; orky-queue-actions.spec.ts + loopback 7 passed / 2 failed (TEST-609/610, same environment class) — frozen TEST-608/612 witnessed green. The vitest gate was and remains unaffected. The proposed CONV (scope zero-invocation stub assertions; run un-gated frozen acceptance suites before review) remains in fix for doc-sync promotion.

claim: TEST-633 (REQ-001's resume half) fails deterministically for a reason UNRELATED to the handshake contamination: the resume gesture does commit exactly one new tile (the toHaveCount(2) step passes), but the PATH-stubbed claude.cmd never executes - claude-log.txt never appears through the 20s poll, identically across all 3 attempts - so the rendered claim 'running claude /orky:resume AT the pane's root' has zero green evidence. The failure pattern points at a possible REAL Windows defect rather than mere harness flake: the launch path spawns the resolved command DIRECTLY via pty.spawn (spawn-spec.ts:25-29 resolves 'claude' through resolveBin's PATHEXT to the .cmd shim and passes it as the PTY process file; pty-manager.ts:27), whereas every previously-green claude-stub e2e (ai-session.spec.ts, minimize-resume.spec.ts) TYPES 'claude' into an interactive shell that resolves and runs the shim itself. If node-pty/ConPTY cannot reliably spawn a .cmd shim directly, resume-in-terminal is broken for the most common Claude install on Windows (the npm .cmd shim) - an F8-shipped path (launchTerminalAt) whose own e2e (the orky-queue-actions.spec.ts claude-stub test, same technique) was also never run green.
fix: Root-cause at the loopback before amending anything: capture the failing pane's rendered PTY output (or reproduce pty.spawn of a bare .cmd outside the app). If the production path is broken, wrap .cmd/.bat resolutions in an explicit cmd.exe /c inside resolveSpawnSpec (a main-process change - escalate/re-attribute this finding to F8's implement phase accordingly); if only the harness technique is at fault, fix the stub technique in the tests-phase loopback (alongside the handshake amendment) and run the test green. Either way TEST-633 must be witnessed green before the gate closes.
note: Re-verified 2026-07-03T02:45:19.278Z: the 04-tests.md determination stands — a BUILD-ENVIRONMENT defect, no repo-code change needed (production spawn path proven sound; node-pty native binding absent: node_modules/node-pty/build/Release contains only obj/, no conpty.node — a rebuild attempt left MSBuild artifacts but no binary; electron-rebuild blocked on MSB8040 Spectre-mitigated libs). Witnessed again at this re-verification: TEST-633 failed at the claude-log poll (orky-pane-actions.spec.ts:339) and F8 TEST-609/610 failed on the identical class, deterministically, while every non-pty test in the same runs passed. Stays OPEN as the environment prerequisite: install the Spectre-mitigated libs, run npx electron-rebuild -f -w node-pty, then witness TEST-633/609/610 green before the review gate closes.

location: src/renderer/components/orky-entry-actions.tsx:87-97 (deliver/aliveRef); src/renderer/components/PaneTile.tsx:158 (hidden={wsInactive || maximizedOver}); 02-spec.md REQ-005 (rendered model's visibility assumption)
claim: The pane mount creates a THIRD outcome-delivery state the shared honesty model does not handle: deliver() uses mount-aliveness (aliveRef) as its user-visibility proxy - unmounted-at-settle routes to the never-dropped store toast chokepoint (CONV-034), mounted-at-settle renders into local phase - but two of the pane's keep-mounted-hidden hosts keep the INITIATING mount MOUNTED with hidden=true (PaneTile.tsx:158: an inactive workspace, since App keeps every workspace mounted; a sibling's maximize-over via .ws-max visibility:hidden), so an answer dispatched from the pane that settles after the user switches workspace or maximizes a sibling renders its outcome into an invisible surface: no toast, no visible signal anywhere in the window. For an INDETERMINATE settle (cli-timeout/ipc-failure - the class whose whole point per CONV-015/CONV-034 is preventing a blind duplicating retry) this is the exact harm: the visible queue mount of the same target honestly returns to idle with re-enabled controls and renders nothing (REQ-005's correct non-initiating model), so the user's only cue that the answer may have landed sits hidden - inviting the duplicate re-dispatch from the queue. F8 never had this state (closing the drawer unmounts -> detached toast); F10's composition into the CONV-028 keep-mounted-hidden hosts is what creates it, and the spec's verified contract cites those hosts for the FETCH discipline while REQ-005 and the networking concern never cover outcome delivery. (The minimize path is exempt: the tile instance unmounts into the MinimizedPaneHost remount, so aliveRef flips and the detached toast fires.)
fix: Treat hidden-at-settle like detached-at-settle at the delivery seam: thread the pane's effective visibility (its hidden prop, or a settle-time store read of the pane's hidden state) into the hook so deliver() ALSO routes through the pushToast chokepoint - same core-classified message, error kind for failures - when the owning surface is effectively hidden; spec the vector and pin it (structural half at the hook seam, rendered half e2e). propose CONV: An outcome-delivery path that uses component mount-aliveness as its user-visibility proxy MUST be re-audited when that component becomes mountable inside a keep-mounted-hidden host (CONV-028's class): an outcome settling while the owning surface is effectively hidden must be reported like a detached outcome, never rendered only into the invisible surface.
resolution: Resolved at the ESC-001 tests loopback + implement fix, re-verified against current code 2026-07-03. OrkyPane threads its OWN hidden prop (both keep-mounted-hidden hosts drive it) into the shared region as the optional hostHidden prop (OrkyPane.tsx:196); the hook reads it at SETTLE time via a ref (orky-entry-actions.tsx:82-83 — never a stale render's closure) and deliver() treats hidden-at-settle like detached-at-settle: the outcome ALSO rides the store toast chokepoint (failure on the never-suppressed 'error' kind, success on the default kind — the F12 precedent), :101-107. The attack verified: a still-mounted-hidden instance KEEPS its setPhase (only the unmounted case returns early at :106), so the pane renders the settled outcome when re-displayed, and exactly ONE toast fires per settle (deliver runs once, in the initiating gesture's continuation; the non-initiating mount attaches none — the toast+phase pair is by-design dual reporting, not a double-toast). The queue mount omits the prop (default false) — F8 byte-safe: orky-entry-actions-structure/-core/-loopback verified byte-unchanged against F8's own test-freeze pins and green. Pinned by TEST-640 (structural, green) and e2e TEST-648 (answer dispatched from the pane, workspace switched mid-flight, outcome toast asserted) — WITNESSED GREEN 2026-07-03.
